plugins.name: commit-conventions
plugins.description: The gitmoji + Conventional Commits standard for commit messages: subject-based scopes, the 50/72 rule, and lower-case-verb casing. Composable with your own conventions.

plugins.name: review-conventions
plugins.description: The Conventional Comments standard for pull and merge request reviews: labelled, decorated, actionable comments. Composable with your own conventions.

plugins.name: solid-principles
plugins.description: The five SOLID principles applied pragmatically: recognizable smells, focused refactorings, and guardrails against speculative abstraction. Composable with your own conventions.

plugins.name: simplicity-principles
plugins.description: The simplicity cluster KISS, DRY, and YAGNI applied pragmatically: do-less heuristics, the rule of three, and the trade-offs such as duplication versus the wrong abstraction. Composable with your own conventions.

plugins.name: clean-code
plugins.description: Clean code craftsmanship applied pragmatically: naming, small focused functions, intent-revealing comments, and disciplined error handling. Composable with your own conventions.

plugins.name: modular-monolith
plugins.description: The modular monolith applied pragmatically: one deployable unit, strictly bounded modules with public APIs, isolated data, and TypeScript-ecosystem boundary enforcement. Composable with your own conventions.

plugins.name: dependency-injection
plugins.description: Dependency injection applied pragmatically: Pure DI (manual constructor injection) first, the composition root, object lifetimes, and the anti-pattern catalogue, with DI containers referenced as a justified option. Composable with your own conventions.

plugins.name: hexagonal-architecture
plugins.description: Hexagonal architecture (ports and adapters) applied pragmatically: the dependency rule, primary and secondary ports and adapters, the application core, composition-root wiring, and its relationship to Onion and Clean Architecture, with TypeScript specifics. Composable with your own conventions.

plugins.name: domain-driven-design
plugins.description: Domain-driven design applied pragmatically, in three skills: EventStorming (collaborative domain discovery, the three formats, the orange-sticky notation), strategic design (ubiquitous language, bounded contexts, subdomains, context mapping), and tactical design (entities, value objects, aggregates, domain events, repositories, domain services, factories), with TypeScript specifics. Composable with your own conventions.
